question.text: When the auction seller fails to deliver securities on the auction pay-in day, the deal will be closed out at:

answer.text: Highest price across exchanges from original trade day till auction day OR 20% above closing price on close-out day, whichever is higher
Average of trade price and auction price

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Failed auction delivery is closed out at the highest price from original trade to auction day or 20% above close-out day closing price, whichever is higher. **The Trap:** This is charged to the auction seller who failed to deliver, not the original defaulting member. **Cross-Exam Context:** Double default (original seller + auction seller) has its own close-out mechanism. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Auction participants should only offer shares they can actually deliver.
